verses.text: Four angels hold the four winds; another seals 'the servants of our God in their foreheads' before harm comes. Sealed. God marks His own before judgment falls.
Four angels hold the four winds; another seals 'the servants of our God in their foreheads' before harm comes. Sealed. God marks His own before judgment falls.
Four angels hold the four winds; another seals 'the servants of our God in their foreheads' before harm comes. Sealed. God marks His own before judgment falls.
The number sealed: 'an hundred and forty and four thousand,' twelve thousand of every tribe of Israel. Full number. None of God's people is missed.
The number sealed: 'an hundred and forty and four thousand,' twelve thousand of every tribe of Israel. Full number. None of God's people is missed.
The number sealed: 'an hundred and forty and four thousand,' twelve thousand of every tribe of Israel. Full number. None of God's people is missed.
The number sealed: 'an hundred and forty and four thousand,' twelve thousand of every tribe of Israel. Full number. None of God's people is missed.
The number sealed: 'an hundred and forty and four thousand,' twelve thousand of every tribe of Israel. Full number. None of God's people is missed.
Then 'a great multitude, which no man could number,' of all nations, in white, cry, 'Salvation to our God... and unto the Lamb.' Countless saved. From every people a vast throng is redeemed.
Then 'a great multitude, which no man could number,' of all nations, in white, cry, 'Salvation to our God... and unto the Lamb.' Countless saved. From every people a vast throng is redeemed.
All the angels fall and worship, saying, 'Blessing, and glory, and wisdom... be unto our God for ever.' Heaven's anthem. Angels join the saved in praise.
All the angels fall and worship, saying, 'Blessing, and glory, and wisdom... be unto our God for ever.' Heaven's anthem. Angels join the saved in praise.
An elder asks who they are: 'these... came out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes... in the blood of the Lamb.' Washed white. The Lamb's blood makes the suffering pure.
An elder asks who they are: 'these... came out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes... in the blood of the Lamb.' Washed white. The Lamb's blood makes the suffering pure.
They serve before the throne; 'God shall wipe away all tears'; the Lamb shall 'feed them, and... lead them unto living fountains.' Comforted. The Lamb shepherds them beyond all sorrow.
